symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden-onset strained/whispered voice with global somatic heaviness
symptoms.onset: abrupt, on returning home from a prolonged event

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute speech disturbance is a stroke-mimicking feature that must be excluded by in-person examination.
Fits the recurrent post-performance crash pattern with vocal fatigue from artificial voice use.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of FAST-positive focal signs (no facial droop, no unilateral weakness, no dysarthria, no dysphagia, no visual loss)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral/global rather than focal symptoms lower the probability of acute stroke, but remote questioning cannot exclude it fully — hence same-day in-person review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptom onset time-locked to prolonged effortful code-switching, relieved by solitude, recurrent pattern

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Situational time-locking and stereotyped recurrence are characteristic of a psychophysiological adjustment reaction once organic causes are excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense/sore anterior neck and laryngeal fatigue after sustained altered voice use; no dyspnoea or drooling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Muscular vocal fatigue from artificial pitch/accent maintenance explains the strained whisper without airway compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Burnout, social isolation and fragmented sense of self; no current suicidal ideation on sensitive enquiry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic minority-stress burnout and identity strain are the psychosocial substrate; mandates mood and self-harm screening.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (BP 115/75, HR 88, RR 14, SpO2 98%, Temp 36.5) and normal neurological examination (symmetric 5/5 power, intact cranial nerves and gag reflex)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician measurement/examination — the normal neuro exam is what actually excludes the mimic in person; it is UNAVAILABLE to a telehealth service, which is why remote escalation for this exam is the correct action.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anterior neck and upper-trapezius muscle tension on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable muscular tension corroborates vocal-fatigue mechanism — a clinician-only finding, sealed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person neurological examination (cranial nerves, symmetric power, gag reflex, fatigability)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The examination that actually excludes TIA/stroke and neuromuscular disease; cannot be done over telehealth, so it defines the same-day in-person escalation.
Reasonable outpatient work-up for fatigue/cognitive slowing if symptoms persist; not an acute telehealth deliverable.
Not something the AI orders remotely; decided in person only if the examination raises concern — over-ordering from chat is inappropriate.
